mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor market conditions signal peak corporate earnings momentum, which sustains positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days before mean reversion sets in.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) combined with rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand-pull inflation and robust corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and INDPRO fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator of consumer confidence and construction sector profitability; a decline in HOUST during tightening cycles predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days as rate sensitivity amplifies valuation compression.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during FEDFUNDS hikes do not produce statistically significant negative 20-day forward returns (t-stat > -1.645), or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) directly reflects aggregate demand strength and corporate hiring expansion, which positively correlates with S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeds 3.5%, the economy operates above potential, triggering inflationary pressures that force the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, ultimately depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.5%, or the negative predictive relationship disappears after adjusting for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production (INDPRO) rises while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains stable signals supply-side efficiency gains without monetary tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-FEDFUNDS divergence signal fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction effect is fully absorbed by a single-series proxy in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.0% YoY), strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) initially supports equity valuations by signaling wage-driven demand, but this effect reverses over a 60-day horizon as higher labor costs compress corporate margins, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth during high CPIAUCSL periods does not predict negative 60-day forward returns, or the sign of the forward return distribution flips to positive after the 30-day mark.
